entry.title: Washington Post: 7 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Severodonetsk defenders holding out under merciless shelling, mayor says
  • Hopes dim, anger grows in British journalist’s disappearance in Brazil
  • Is Pope Francis nearing the end of his pontificate?
  • Kyiv asks for more rocket systems as Kremlin warns of potential strikes in Russia
  • Australians gag as KFC uses cabbage for lettuce in burgers amid shortages
  • What to know about Severodonetsk, the Ukrainian city Russia wants to capture
  • British tourist sentenced to 15 years in Iraqi prison for taking artifacts
  • Sophie Freud, professor who challenged her grandfather’s doctrine, dies at 97
  • Lilibet turns 1, and photos show Harry and Meghan’s daughter loving it
  • China’s university entrance exams are already stressful. Now add covid.
  • The Trump-like TikTok star who could be Colombia’s next president
  • ‘Needle spiking’ fears rise in Europe, but crime ‘really difficult’ to trace
  • Millions of dollars went missing as Afghanistan fell, watchdog says
  • Fighting in Severodonetsk has ‘worsened,’ official says; Britain pledges rocket systems
  • India scrambles to contain fallout over insulting comments about Islam
  • The world’s largest four-day workweek pilot just launched in the U.K.
  • The diplomatic war over Ukrainian grain exports heats up
  • Ukraine’s position has ‘worsened’ in fight for Severodonetsk
  • Cholera fears prompt quarantine in Mariupol, official says
  • Boris Johnson survives but is weakened by no-confidence vote
  • Mexican footbridge collapses as mayor inaugurates it
  • Police release sketch of girl found in Toronto dumpster
  • Migrant caravan on the move in southern Mexico
  • Report: 'Perpetual' Israeli occupation at root of violence
  • Dutch prosecutors demand life sentences in reporter slaying
  • Trump call Jan. 6 to ‘walk down to the Capitol’ prompted Secret Service scramble
  • Teen killed in ’70s may have been victim of serial killer, detectives say
  • Minn. man impersonating federal agent on TikTok sentenced to six years
  • Mother charged after 2-year-old finds gun, fatally shoots his father
  • Man drowns as Arizona police watch: ‘I’m not jumping in after you’
  • She found $36,000 inside a chair she got for free on Craigslist
  • Jan. 6 hearing will preempt ‘Young Sheldon’ — but not Tucker Carlson
  • Primary elections live updates: Voters in California, six other states to decide primaries
  • Mexico’s president snubs Biden’s invitation to summit
  • Proud Boys leader Tarrio, 4 lieutenants charged with seditious conspiracy
  • Post Politics Now: Jan. 6, guns on the radar for Congress; Biden announces latest baby formula flight
  • World Bank warns global economy may suffer 1970s-style stagflation
  • Target warns of profit drop, moves to purge excess inventory
  • Raytheon will move its headquarters to Arlington
  • Crypto industry scores a big win under long-anticipated Senate bill
  • Meet the YouTubers exposing the dark side of making video games
  • E3 video game convention will return in 2023, says parent company
  • How to screen remote-learning apps for privacy
  • Townhouse community is close to beach but still ‘feels very private’
  • How to bring stamped concrete back to life
  • Contemporary, 300-unit apartment complex opens in Alexandria
  • How Well Is Joe Biden Actually Doing?
  • New York City Is a Lot Safer Than Small-Town America
  • JPMorgan Stars Take Other Side of Dimon’s ‘Hurricane’ Trade
  • Target’s Oversupply Problem Should Scare All Retailers
  • What the Jan. 6 Committee Has Done, and What’s Next
  • Meet the Guptas, Symbols of South African Corruption
  • How a Battery Metals Squeeze Puts EV Future at Risk
  • What’s Behind Vietnam’s Latest Anti-Corruption Fight
  • Trump is gambling big in Wisconsin and Arizona. Losses there could be painful.
  • This Russian journalist blew up her life to protest Putin’s war. Now she’s in limbo.
  • Is there a sickness in U.S. culture? Yes: The GOP gun cult.
  • What inflation struggles of the past can tell the Fed right now
  • Why Black people are afraid of ‘crazy’ White people
  • The Jan. 6 committee’s audience won’t match Watergate’s. But it should.
  • We already achieved ‘energy independence.’ What good did it do us?
  • Vladimir Kara-Murza from jail: The worst nightmare for political prisoners
  • Eric Holder on the violent past and imperiled future of voting
  • Myanmar’s military must not hang those fighting for democracy
  • The government is finally tackling ransomware. More work remains.
  • Ignore the GOP spin. The nation still hasn’t reckoned with Jan. 6.
  • Why is trust in government so low? There’s blame to go around.
  • How Fox News has dominated the White House briefing
  • John Fetterman shows the challenge Democrats face in middle America
  • The world is finally reacting to India’s descent into hate
  • At the Summit of the Americas, listen to the region’s youngest leader
  • Goldman lost his Virginia House district suit, but the door is open
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • What are the Democrats’ biggest problems? Chat with Perry Bacon Jr.
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 7 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Global Growth Will Be Choked Amid Inflation and War, Says World Bank
  • Yellen Defends Pandemic Spending as Inflation Persists
  • Ukrainian Officials Move to Rechristen Landmarks With Russian Names
  • AOC Backs Challenge to Sean Maloney, Who Also Leads Democrats’ Campaign Strategy
  • Jan. 6 Hearings Give Democrats a Chance to Recast Midterm Message
  • Fox News Doesn’t Plan to Carry Jan. 6 Hearings Live
  • The Jan. 6 Inquiry’s Only Endangered Democrat Prepares Herself for a Fight

  • How a Crisis Actor Conspiracy Theory Rises in Times of Tragedy

  • North Korea’s Covid Outbreak Is Scary, and an Opportunity
  • Thomas Piketty’s Case for ‘Participatory Socialism’

  • What Do You Fear the Most? ‘That I’m Not Who I Should Be.’
  • Boris Johnson Is at the Beginning of the End
  • The Global Minimum Tax Deal Is in Trouble

  • Terrence Real’s Latest Book Explores Fixing What Ails Us

  • Before Chickens Were Nuggets, They Were Revered
  • Ukraine and Russia Exchange War Dead Remains
  • Democratic Lawmakers Urge Biden to Ensure Saudi Ties Serve U.S.

  • Your Tuesday Briefing
  • British Journalist and Brazilian Indigenous Expert Missing in the Amazon
  • How ‘Trustless’ Is Bitcoin, Really?
  • Potent Weapons Reach Ukraine Faster Than the Know-How to Use Them
  • A Conservative Quits Georgetown’s Law School Amid Free Speech Fight
  • Big City Mayors, Furious About Mass Shootings, Fear Sweeping Gun Limits Are Out of Reach
  • A Broken Redistricting Process Winds Down, With No Repairs in Sight
  • Karen Bass Faces a Battle With Rick Caruso for Los Angeles Mayor
  • California Voters Have Top Two Open Primary Election on Tuesday
  • Mississippi: How to Vote, Where to Vote and What’s on the Ballot
  • South Dakota: How to Vote, Where to Vote and What’s on the Ballot
  • Montana: How to Vote, Where to Vote and What’s on the Ballot Today
  • Iowa: How to Vote, Where to Vote and What’s on the Ballot
  • New Mexico: How to Vote, Where to Vote and What’s on the Ballot
  • California: How to Vote, Where to Vote and What’s on the Ballot
  • New Jersey: How to Vote, Where to Vote and What’s on the Ballot
  • California June Primary Voter Turnout Expected to Be Low
  • Mother Charged With Manslaughter After 2-Year-Old Fatally Shoots Father
  • Louisiana House Map Is Blocked by Judge Who Calls It a Racial Gerrymander
  • Jessica Cisneros Calls for Recount in Texas Runoff Against Rep. Cuellar
  • Democrats Weigh a Comeback Strategy in Factory Towns
  • Arizona Officers on Leave After Watching Man Struggle and Drown
  • Proud Boys Charged With Sedition in Capitol Attack
  • How Updates in iOS 16 and Android 13 Will Change Your Phone
  • Shell, the Oil Giant, Will Sell Renewable Energy to Texans
  • Target will take a profit hit to clear out inventory that shoppers don’t want.
  • Biden to Pause New Solar Tariffs as White House Aims to Boost Adoption
  • Philadelphia Museum of Art Names a New Director
  • E.U. Rule Requires New Devices to Have USB-C Charging Ports by 2026
  • Texas Enters the Musk-Twitter Fray With Paxton Investigation
  • Target Will Cut Prices to Clear Out Unwanted Inventory, Taking a Profit Hit
  • F.D.A. Advisers Meet on Covid Shots by Novavax
  • Companies Like Nexii Are Betting on Sustainable Construction
  • Wells Fargo Announces ‘Pause’ of Policy That Led to Fake Job Interviews
  • Roman Abramovich Is the Target of Court Filings to Seize Two Planes
  • Axon Pauses Plans for Taser Drone as Ethics Board Members Resign
  • Apple Unveils Software Updates for iOS 16
  • Elon Musk Threatens to End Twitter Deal Without Information on Spam Accounts

entry.title: Guardian: 7 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Germany v England: Nations League – live!
  • United Arab Emirates v Australia: World Cup 2022 qualifying playoff – live!
  • Uvalde native Matthew McConaughey says ‘real change can happen’ on gun reform – live
  • Russia-Ukraine war: almost 600 people ‘detained in torture chambers’ in Kherson, says Ukraine – live
  • Children of shot crime reporter confront accused in Dutch court
  • Russian assault on Sievierodonetsk redoubled after counterattack
  • Jim Seals, of soft rock duo Seals and Crofts, dies aged 80
  • The reef fish people find ugly more likely to be endangered, study finds

  • High-stakes California races will decide LA mayor and San Francisco recall
  • First openly trans NFL cheerleader to make her debut in 2022 season
  • Spain fights food waste with supermarket fines and doggy bags
  • Birmingham restaurant flooded with calls after Johnny Depp visit
  • Florida cop’s hunch links six cold cases to accused 80s ‘pillowcase rapist’
  • Matilda: the cake-based torture sticks in the mind – but this delightful film is worth rewatching

  • Skateboard hero of 2017 London Bridge attack proposed for sainthood
  • Rebel Tories plan ‘vote strikes’ to capitalise on PM’s weakened position
  • Eleven members of Rome-based mafia clan face trial over electricity theft
  • Israel’s coalition on brink of collapse after losing settler law vote
  • Fake Trump electors told to operate in ‘complete secrecy’, email reveals
  • Johnson must show he is useful to his party, or he will quickly lose his fight for survival | Katy Balls
  • Colbert on the January 6 TV hearings: ‘Your new favorite show: America’s Got Treason’
  • China accuses Australia and Canada of ‘disinformation’ over jet encounters
  • UK cinema chain cancels screenings of ‘blasphemous’ film after protests
  • England fans arrested for Nazi salutes and hotel room damage in Munich
  • There may be trouble ahead for Boris Johnson despite confidence vote win
  • Hit send too soon? Now you’ll be able to edit those ill-advised iMessages
  • Real Madrid agree €100m deal to sign Aurelién Tchouaméni from Monaco
  • Brad Pitt accuses Angelina Jolie of seeking to ‘inflict harm’ on him over vineyard
  • Why was the jubilee a success? Because republicans had nothing as jolly to offer | Stephen Bates
  • The Righteous review – thoughtful horror is soaked in Catholic guilt
  • ‘The story enraged me’ – US artist Deborah Roberts on her Child Q collage
  • Will anti-abortionists use ‘uterus surveillance’ against women in the US? | Arwa Mahdawi
  • Emma Raducanu has ‘no idea’ if she will play at Wimbledon after freak injury
  • Chefs’ tips on their favourite summer party cakes | Kitchen aide
  • Only cultural change will free America from its gun problem | Andrew Gawthorpe
  • Rome’s cultural elite write to Unesco over ‘mortifying’ scenes of rubbish
  • ‘I know I’m not going to please everyone’: Lucile Hadžihalilović on her beguiling film-making
  • Spanish police investigate 20,000 undelivered letters from decade ago
  • With Hannibal Johnson safe in his job for now, the Tory party can carry on devouring itself | Marina Hyde
  • How can I tell my husband I want us to have sex?
  • The best albums of 2022 so far
  • Play some Kylie, keep it short and enlist your friends! The beginner’s guide to loving exercise
  • Kate Bush earns first ever US Top 10 hit with Running Up That Hill
  • Kangaroos in the street and a suitcase of iguanas: India’s exotic pet problem
  • Funding needed for climate disasters has risen ‘more than 800%’ in 20 years
  • Dead dolphins: how nature became another casualty of the Ukraine war
  • EU faces legal challenge over plan to fast-track gas projects
  • Polio outbreak in Pakistan worsens as eighth child reported paralysed
  • How do you get 20m tonnes of grain out of Ukraine?
  • Russian-language Ukrainian TV channel aims to topple Putin
  • Federal judge authorizes US to seize two of Roman Abramovich’s private jets
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.content:

entry.title: Financial Times: 7 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Ukraine’s Zelenskyy says stalemate with Russia ‘not an option’
  • Volodymyr Zelenskyy: ‘No one is humiliating Ukraine. They are killing us’
  • Germany backs Baltic demands for more Nato troops

  • Do I have to disclose rental income on my tax return?

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 7 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 7 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-07 17:00PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Changes needed to Hong Kong public housing allocation policy to help cut waiting times for most needy, government advisers says
  • Hong Kong proposes banning CBD products within year, after one third of such items sold in city found to contain traces of illegal active ingredient in marijuana
  • Cathay Pacific given access to HK$7.8 billion loan from Hong Kong government for another year
  • Elderly man struck by lightning in Hong Kong as Observatory warns of serious flooding
  • Malaysian professor loses appeal against life sentence for yoga ball murders in Hong Kong
  • Hong Kong remains world’s most expensive city for expats for third straight year, city becoming ‘less attractive’ than regional rivals
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong snack chain Aji Ichiban shuts all 20 retail outlets amid losses from lack of tourists during pandemic
  • Life in Hong Kong’s shoebox housing - complete visual explainer
  • Heavy rain exposes dummy grenade sparking bomb scare at Hong Kong army barracks
  • Hong Kong tram fares to rise to HK$3, a steeper increase than expected, as operator struggles with fall in ridership and shrinking revenue
  • Hong Kong’s self-styled ‘Captain America’ spouted ‘empty words’, legal counsel says in first appeal related to city’s national security law
  • Tickets for Hong Kong Palace Museum to cost between HK$50 and HK$120, with venue to open on July 2
  • Out-of-control truck pins police car against barrier, crashes into 2 vehicles during Hong Kong morning rush hour
  • Nan Hai says creditors are freezing its assets as it comes up short to meet US$350 million of notes due this weekend
  • Hong Kong extends regulatory rules to NFT-based collective investment schemes, warning investors to be mindful of risks
  • Hong Kong leader Carrie Lam defends police action to close off Victoria Park on June 4, saying anniversary of Tiananmen Square crackdown could spur violent activism
  • ESF kindergartens provide the perfect environment for young learners to flourish and grow
  • Coronavirus: too early to suggest arrival of sixth wave, Hong Kong leader Carrie Lam says, as she vows to facilitate arrival of state leaders
  • Hong Kong counterterrorism unit set to launch hotline for tip-offs on suspicious activities with cash rewards for useful information
  • Hong Kong police to mobilise all officers for possible state leader visit at handover bash, force chief says
  • Hong Kong police chief warns viewers to avoid documentary about city’s social unrest over concerns of possible national security violations
  • Taiwan air force jet makes hard landing in US after equipment failure
  • China’s ‘economic momentum’ fuelling June’s GDP growth, skewing quarterly estimates
  • China says all IAEA member states must agree before Aukus nuclear sub project begins
  • China’s Universal Studios to partially reopen after six-week closure due to Covid controls in Beijing
  • China’s regulators exclude Tencent, NetEase as they approve 60 online game titles in June
  • Ant Group emerges as ‘less finance and more tech’ two years after mega IPO was called off, analysts say in wake of ESG report
  • China and US locked in new infowar after Chinese social media claims American manipulation over Xinjiang
  • US coffee giant Starbucks faces more backlash in China over expired products
  • Chinese top influencer Austin Li Jiaqi’s absence imperils live-streaming campaigns of Taobao, various brands for 618 festival
  • Japan and China ‘must think long term to improve ties’ as frictions rise over Taiwan
  • ‘Please don’t forget’: Taiwan appeals to US for free-trade deal
  • Why do giraffes have long necks? It could have been to fight for mates
  • China’s forex reserves climb for the first time in 2022, but yuan depreciation pressure remains
  • China’s falling birth rate prompts Elon Musk to warn of looming ‘population collapse’
  • Lockdowns and Tiananmen amnesia – why Beijing isn’t ready for the Chinese century
  • Can Joe Biden’s economic package overcome Americas summit exclusion row?
  • China’s 30/60 goals: Beijing unveils ‘transition bonds’ for eight dirty industries, to meet carbon reduction targets
  • Gruelling gaokao exams put China’s ‘epidemic generation’ to the test
  • China plans to start building first-ever solar power plant in space by 2028
  • China plans sweeping makeover of academic journals to raise the profile and influence of domestic scientific research
  • Kuomintang chairman, in Washington, pitches his party as stabilising presence between Beijing and Taipei
  • China’s foreign minister in Kazakhstan as Ukraine, Afghanistan uncertainties rumble
  • China’s unemployment woes see Yunnan offer big cash subsidies to entice college graduates
  • Chinese scientists say ‘Midas’ touch helps to fine-tune CRISPR gene editing
  • EU lawmakers push to designate human rights conditions in Xinjiang as ‘genocide’
  • India’s ruling party asks officials to exercise caution on religious issues after Islamic nations protest
  • Indonesian colonel sentenced to life in prison for killing teenagers
  • Japan, Nato strengthen ties amid Russia’s invasion of Ukraine – step up military action
  • Myanmar junta’s ‘cold blooded assassination’: rights groups condemn decision to hang political prisoners
  • Pacific tensions: New Zealand leaders call for less ‘patronising’ approach to island nations
  • Thailand urged to let refugee status of stranded Rohingya be evaluated by UN
  • What caused a Malaysian man to slice his mother into 15 pieces in alleged murder?
  • US, S Korea fly 20 fighter jets over N Korea in ‘swift and forceful’ response, a day after warning
  • Oscar-winning Japanese musician ‘The Last Emperor’ Sakamoto battling stage IV cancer
  • US warns of ‘forceful response’ to anticipated North Korea nuclear test
  • Tourists to Japan may be sent home if they don’t wear face masks and follow other health measures, government says
  • India under fire as Muslim nations lash out over Prophet Mohammed remarks
  • Australia’s lettuce shortage forces KFC to put cabbage in its burgers
  • Singapore airfares cost 27 per cent more than before Covid-19 – so why are prices so high?
  • China ‘testing’ Tokyo with underwater survey off Japan’s coast, analysts say
  • New ‘doggy bag’ law for bars and restaurants in Spain set to be enforced in order to limit waste
  • French health workers warn of ‘deadly chaos’ in protest over pay, just days before elections
  • Electronic device chargers set to all be the same – no more tangled cables and less waste, EU says
  • Donald Trump reposts edited image of his likeness on Mount Rushmore, next to George Washington
  • Ukraine war: Russia bans 61 more Americans as US media face visa, accreditation denial
  • Blinken says reports of Russia stealing Ukraine grain for profit ‘credible’
  • Ukraine’s Zelensky says Russian troops intend to capture Zaporizhzhia, after fall of Kherson and Mariupol
  • ‘Greased piglet’ Boris Johnson survives as UK prime minister. For now
  • UK reports 77 new monkeypox cases, biggest outbreak outside Africa
  • Shock as Iraq jails British man Jim Fitton for 15 years over smuggling artefacts
  • Gupta brothers accused of defrauding South Africa arrested in UAE
  • Two-year-old US boy shoots and kills father after finding gun at home
  • UK’s Boris Johnson survives no-confidence vote after partygate rebellion
  • Taser-maker Axon halts controversial project to equip drones with stun guns
  • Five key real estate priorities for Asia Pacific companies, and what this means for the post-pandemic office of the future
  • Evergrande loses ally after state-owned China Railway puts its stake in real estate unit up for sale
  • Shanghai reopening: world’s biggest port reaps benefits of higher freight rates as exporters get back on their feet after lockdown
  • Investors brand Greenland’s bond payment extension unfair as credibility of state-backed companies comes into question
  • How 3 insurance professionals have helped Hong Kong tackle challenges such as Covid-19 pandemic
  • Citic Securities tops China’s brokerage industry charts, beating peers in assets, revenue and profit
  • LME in court again, faces US$15.3 million suit from trading firm Jane Street Global for cancelling nickel futures trades
  • Why Shanghai reopening is good for Biden and global economy
  • Hong Kong landlords and sellers should brace themselves for a more realistic property market in the near term
  • Traditional bricks-and-mortar toolmaker turns to digital commerce platform Vtex to help it on its digitalisation journey
  • Hong Kong stocks slip from two-month high as Covid-19 cases rebound while Chinese tech firms surrender gains
  • Tencent, Alibaba backed Chinese start-ups G7 Connect, E6 Technology merge to create truck management services giant
  • Hong Kong real estate owners seek alternative uses for retail complexes and hotels amid tourism slump
  • Climate change: UK-backed initiative aims to tackle greenwashing with globally recognised emissions standards
  • What Singapore can teach Canada about curbing home prices without the xenophobia
  • Chinese carmakers get V-sign from analysts as tax cuts seen reviving share prices, sales from April slump
  • Disney+ K-drama Doctor Lawyer: So Ji-sub returns in punchy medical-legal drama mash-up
  • Why are morel mushrooms so loved by Hong Kong chefs? They’re bursting with savoury flavour and full of amino acids and antioxidants
  • Queer artist Oscar yi Hou blends Asian and American imagery to explore ideas of migration, identity and belonging
  • Lamborghini, Ferrari, Maserati, Ducati and Pagani luxury sports cars and bikes all come from the same part of Italy – a visit to Motor Valley
  • How endangered languages in countries like Indonesia and Malaysia are a vital tool in saving the marine environment
  • Pride Month 2022: how do you talk about LGBTQ issues? Better education would go a long way, says Hong Kong rights advocate
  • When Stephen Hawking visited Hong Kong in 2006, famed scientist had a rock-star welcome at the airport
  • Hong Kong has always had foreign residents who found the city incredibly boring
  • 10 summer health tips from Ayurveda experts to avoid excess body heat and stay healthy
  • 2022 LVMH Prize jury rewarded young fashion designers, and Cate Blanchett, Louis Vuitton’s Nicolas Ghesquière and Delphine Arnault were there with advice and support
  • Who is Stranger Things 4’s Joseph Quinn? Playing season newbie Eddie Munson, the 29-year-old British actor also appeared in Game of Thrones and hopes to star in Lord of the Rings
  • Move over, Scott Disick! Meet Sofia Richie’s new fiancé, Elliot Grainge: his dad is CEO of Universal Music Group and he founded his own label, 10K Projects – but do Lionel and Nicole approve?
  • The most expensive Kardashian-Jenner properties, ranked: from Kim, Kris, Khloé and Kylie’s Hidden Hills homes, to multimillion-dollar mansions shared with exes Kanye West and Travis Scott
  • Not just for royals: Queen Elizabeth and Meghan Markle don tiaras of course, but now so do Blake Lively, Madonna, Angelababy and other A-listers on the red carpet …
  • K-drama queens who don’t look a day over 25: Jang Na-ra, Jun Ji-hyun, Ha Ji-won, Go Hyun-jung and Han Go-eun share their anti-ageing beauty, fitness and diet tips – here’s how they do it
  • Inside Sourav Ganguly’s new US$5 million family home: the ‘Prince of Kolkata’ and Indian cricket legend splashed out on his first move in 48 years from his hometown of Behala
  • How royal titles will change when Prince Charles becomes king: Queen Elizabeth just celebrated her Platinum Jubilee, but what will William, Harry and Kate Middleton be called in future?
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.content:

entry.content:

  • VOX POPULI: Meteorite soil may show the ‘umami of life’ came from space
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
